decision_target: Preserve the independently accepted v3 bounded queue behavior while replacing the validator's self-matching source audit with an explicit evidence-universe rule.
source_of_truth: The byte-identical v3 model and queue contract, the eight-case behavior probe, accepted DKR-2 discovery artifact, current Lite exports, frame v2, and validator-dkr-4-v3's accepted behavior findings plus checker-self-match rejection.
read_method: Run bash .okra/runs/issue-triage-session-20260715/workers/dkr-4-v4/replay.sh from the feature root and require eight cases, five ports, three controller edges, accepted DKR-2 discovery with implementation false, ten hashes, eight fresh walls, the public checkpoint contract, and a source audit limited to the named candidate and Lite export allowlist.

confidence_probability_update.reason: Validator-dkr-4-v3 accepted every candidate behavior and contract fact; its only causal failure was scanning its own assertion fixture string.

questions_answered: The v3 behavior remains eight of eight cases, concurrency two, one activation per lease, graceful join in every case, and zero crossed session values.
The byte-identical v3 model still has five required ports, three controller edges, five declared effect edges, and no forbidden public lifecycle surface.
DKR-2 remains accepted_as_reducing_discovery while implementation_authorized remains false.
Validator-dkr-4-v3 accepted the candidate behavior, graph, lifecycle boundary, hashes, public contract, and product boundary.
Validator-dkr-4-v3 rejected its independent checker because the checker scanned its own spawnSync assertion fixture; the wall-gate rejection was derivative of that missing independent replay.

The v4 source audit scans only named candidate surface fields and product Lite exported symbols; validator source, checker source, fixture literals, and assertion source are outside the evidence universe.
The checkpoint and all eight wall records use frame max_age 10m.
questions_unanswered: A separate validator has not accepted v4.
DKR-2 has no Lite implementation, so forced context close remains outside current queue behavior.
Provider acknowledgement idempotency, lease fencing, receive shutdown, and distributed fairness remain adapter concerns.
decision: candidate only; preserve v3 bounded controller behavior and corrected facts byte-for-byte or semantically, repair only the validator audit universe, and await independent v4 acceptance
flag_if_missing_or_stale: cannot: fail closed when any behavior case, graph edge, evidence hash, DKR-2 state, ten-minute wall read, public contract check, named source allowlist, or independent v4 review is missing, stale, wrong-source, non-replayable, or contradicted

validator_guidance.rule: Audit only the named candidate artifact fields and product Lite exported symbols. Never scan validator source, checker source, fixtures, or assertion literals as candidate evidence.

repairs_validator_harness_rejection.repair: State and replay an explicit source-audit allowlist; assertion and fixture source are outside the evidence universe.

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: Five required ports and three controller edges in the byte-identical v3 modeled surface
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Count effects or child calls outside required ports and declared controller edges.

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: Five declared v3 effect edges and the allowlisted modeled surface
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Require every modeled effect edge to carry via_required_port true.

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: The accepted two-session one-Scope behavior trace
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Compare session, issue, lease, observation, and result ownership across both activations.

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: In-memory probe adapters and validator-dkr-4-v3's accepted product boundary
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Count real GitHub, queue, process, network, database, or product writes.

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: Eight queue behaviors, fixed loop, five ports, three edges, and accepted DKR-2 discovery boundary
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Replay admission, backpressure, join, ownership, and shutdown without a public lifecycle or pool surface.

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: Validator-dkr-4-v3's independent rejection and v4's candidate-only status
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Confirm no writer acceptance exists and require a separate v4 validator.

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: Ten named evidence hashes, governed worker report, and store verification
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Resolve every hash, append through the absolute helper, and verify the run store.

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: The preserved v3 checker-self-match rejection, explicit v4 audit universe, and blocked gate
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Confirm the rejection remains cited as a validator-harness self-match only, no assertion source is scanned, and no writer acceptance is recorded.

wall_gate.reason: All eight current candidate reads are held, but independent v4 acceptance and DKR-2 implementation authority remain absent.

empty_result_policy: An empty result is a valid non-accepted decision that still carries a replayable probe trace.
checkpoint_rejection_policy: Superficial, tautological, false-positive, fabricated, unsupported, and single-LLM-truth checkpoints are rejected. Missing, stale, wrong-source, non-replayable, or contradicted evidence fails closed and opens a blocking flag.

worked_checkpoint_trace.decision: candidate only pending independent v4 replay

role_grammar: CKRs are measurable contribution context, not worker work.
DKRs are discovery-worker scopes; PKRs are progression-worker execution units; there is no CKR worker.
